Bombarnac boards the Grand Transasiatic bound for Peking, hunting stories among his fellow travelers, for "either one is a correspondent or one is not!" (ASC-001-035). His richest discovery is a secret: hidden in a packing case in the baggage van rides the young Roumanian Kinko, a stowaway crossing Asia to rejoin his sweetheart (ASC-015-003).
- The *Dolphin* — the swift Clyde-built steamer designed to outrun the Federal blockaders

[[captain-nemo|Nemo]]'s death resolves the mystery that has guided the colonists' survival from the first, disclosing that the unseen hand which saved them again and again was that of the exile of *Twenty Thousand Leagues*. That he chose the *[[nautilus|Nautilus]]* as his grave (MI-059-025) unites the two novels in a single figure, and Harding's benediction over [[captain-nemo|Captain Nemo]], our benefactor (MI-062-037) transforms the enigmatic outlaw of the sea into a redeemed protector.

Utah is the Mormon territory of the American West, crossed by the Pacific Railroad in [[Around the World in Eighty Days]]. As Phileas Fogg's train pressed westward it "penetrated Utah, the region of the Great Salt Lake, the singular colony of the Mormons" (AWED-026-026), a land apart within the young Union.

Connecticut is a New England state of the United States. In the Vernean corpus it is most closely tied to *An Antarctic Mystery*, where it is the home country of the narrator Jeorling, who repeatedly names it as the place he means to return to after his voyage into the southern ice. It reappears as a stretch of the New England coast in *The Master of the World* and as the site of a notable tempest in *Robur the Conqueror*.

The train "extends to the station of Douchak at the six hundred and sixtieth verst, which we reach at six o'clock in the morning," (ASC-008-022) and there the reporter sets off "to look at Douchak with Major Noltitz as my cicerone." (ASC-008-023) The two "did" the town together, (ASC-008-028) though "the stoppage at Douchak had another half hour to last," (ASC-008-060) and afterwards the travellers found "the provisions having come freshly on board at Askhabad and Douchak." The Wreck of the Dream is the foundering of the steam yacht *Dream* on a Pacific reef, the disaster that strands Godfrey Morgan and his tutor Tartlet as castaways and sets in motion the robinsonade of *[[godfrey-morgan|Godfrey Morgan]]*.
